subject: MATHEMATICS
title: Recognize, draw, and describe points, lines, line
objective: Recognize, draw, and describe points, lines, line segments, and rays using models.
introduction: Look around you! The tip of your pencil is a point. The edge of your ruler is a line segment. The sun's rays shooting through the window — those are rays! Geometry is everywhere. Today you'll become a Geometry Explorer and learn to spot points, lines, line segments, and rays in the world around you.

vocabulary.definition: Isang eksaktong lokasyon, tulad ng dulo ng lapis.
Isang tuwid na guhit na walang hangganan sa magkabilang dulo.
Bahagi ng linya na may dalawang dulo.

activities.instruction: Watch and learn! See how points, lines, and rays are different.
activities.content: A POINT is a tiny dot showing an exact spot. A LINE SEGMENT connects two points — it stops! A RAY starts at one point and goes on forever. PARALLEL lines never cross, like train tracks. PERPENDICULAR lines cross at a perfect corner.

feedback.correct: Now you know the basics! Let's practice.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text description of geometric shapes: point, line segment, ray, parallel, perpendicular.
activities.narration: Milo says: Let's learn about this! Pay close attention.

activities.instruction: Tap each shape to learn its name and what makes it special!

examples: Point A — a tiny red dot showing an exact location
Line Segment CD — a blue line with two endpoints. It stops!
Ray EF — an orange line with one endpoint. It goes forever!
Parallel Lines — two purple lines that never meet
Perpendicular Lines — two green lines that cross at a square corner

feedback.retry: Tap the remaining shapes to learn about them.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Five geometric shapes are listed: point, line segment, ray, parallel lines, perpendicular lines.
activities.prompt: Find and tap the correct place.
activities.narration: Can you spot it? Tap the correct spot!

activities.instruction: Sort each item: is it a POINT, LINE SEGMENT, or RAY?

fits: Edge of a book — LINE SEGMENT
Side of a door — LINE SEGMENT

feedback.correct: You sorted them all! You're thinking like a geometer.
feedback.retry: Some items need a different group.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Sort real-world examples into point, line segment, or ray categories.
activities.prompt: Drag each item to its group.
activities.narration: Sort these into groups! What belongs together?

options: A straight path with two endpoints
A shape that goes on forever in both directions
A round shape with no corners

feedback.correct: Correct! A line segment has two endpoints — it stops!
feedback.retry: Think: does it have endpoints or go forever?

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Choose which description matches a line segment.
activities.narration: Choose the best answer! You can do it!

activities.instruction: Match each geometry word to its real-world example!

pairs.right: A sunbeam coming through the window
The two rails of a train track

feedback.correct: Perfect matches! Geometry is all around you.
feedback.retry: Try matching each word to the best example.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Match geometry terms to real-world examples.
activities.prompt: Match the ones that go together!
activities.narration: Match the pairs! Which ones go together?

activities.instruction: Put these in order: from simplest to most complex!

feedback.correct: That's the right order — from point to parallel lines!
feedback.retry: Start with the simplest idea first.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Order geometric concepts from simplest to most complex.
activities.narration: What's the correct order? Drag to arrange!

storyIntro: Milo is counting his toys. "Numbers are everywhere! Let's figure this out!"
